message:
fix(scanner): cap per-dir iteration at 2000 entries + deferred child dispatch

Two production issues fixed:

1. Performance: directories with 8K-60K+ entries (e.g. /data/tmp, target/release/deps/)
   caused the walker to spend seconds per directory iterating entries that contribute
   no useful signals. Structural markers (.fingerprint, deps, incremental) appear within
   the first few hundred entries. Cap at 2000 entries reduces per-directory cost from
   50-100ms to ~1ms. Result: css scan 14K entries in 0.5s (was 103 entries in 60s timeout).

2. Race condition: child directories were queued to worker threads during iteration,
   before .sbh-protect markers (which appear at any position in the listing) were
   discovered. Another thread could process a child before the marker was found.
   Fix: collect children in a Vec during iteration, dispatch to work queue only after
   confirming no marker exists (deferred dispatch pattern). Test now passes 20/20
   (was 3/10 flaky).

Deployed as v4 to all 11 production machines. Scanner completes full passes in 0.3-0.5s
across 13K-16K entries with 0 timeouts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
